title: GKA (Local Policy)
description: This policy governs the rules of conduct for members of the public when they are on school district property or attending school-sponsored events. It applies to community members, visitors, and anyone who interacts with the district in a public setting. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it outlines expectations for behavior on campus, including an updated ban on tobacco products, e-cigarettes, and electronic vaporizing devices, helping ensure a safe and healthy environment for students and staff.

timeline.summary: The Tobacco and E-Cigarettes section of GKA (Local Policy) was updated to expand the list of prohibited items. Specifically, 'electronic vaporizing devices' were added alongside e-cigarettes in the prohibition on smoking and tobacco product use on District property, in District vehicles, or at school-related activities.
timeline.discussion:
** Insert the word "rules" after "state" (referencing State Board of Education rules on dyslexia education).
**VOTE on amendment: Approved 7-0**
**VOTE on EHB as amended: Approved 7-0**

**⚠️ TABLED: GKA Local** — Tabled until definitions regarding tobacco, caffeine, and nicotine can be clarified; to return with enforceable language.
**VOTE to table: Approved 7-0**

**Remaining policies approved as a group:** BBE, CA, CDA, CQB, EHB, GKB (all local).
**VOTE: Approved 7-0**

## Closed Session

timeline.summary: This page shows the text of Conroe ISD's local policy GKA (Community Relations: Conduct on School Premises), dated 2021-01-14, Version 1. It covers access to district property, ejection or exclusion under Education Code 37.105, off-campus activities, and begins a section on prohibitions. No redline changes (underlined additions or strikethrough removals) are visible on this page — it appears to be a clean policy text page, possibly part of a policy update packet (Policy Update Page Number: 30).
timeline.discussion:
; "placement" replaces "selection."
- Added exit provisions and appeals process language.
- Funding provision updated: state provides 5% and district may spend 100% (previously more restricted).

### GKA Local — Community Relations/Conduct on School Premises
- Updates regarding advertising consistency with district values.
- Adds e-cigarette/vaporizer devices to prohibited items on premises.
- **Trustee noted inconsistency:** Previous policy (DH) uses "nicotine" while this uses "tobacco." Requested language be made consistent — Dr. Vinson to research and update for second reading.
